messages.content: Antoine Stargaze was born in 1884 to a middle-class family. Unbeknownst to him, he has immortality, and his aging will stop in 1900.; Timeline ends in 1891.

Since the timeline ends in 1891 and Antoine Stargaze hasn't reached the year 1900 yet, he would be about seven years away from the point when his aging is supposed to stop due to his immortality. At this stage in the timeline, he would be a 7-year-old boy, likely unaware of his unique trait of immortality. Given the time period, his family's middle-class status suggests that he might have access to basic education and healthcare, typical for that era and social class.

If we are to continue the fictional story of Antoine growing up and eventually realizing that his aging has stopped in 1900, he would be 16 years physically forever. As the years passed, he would witness monumental changes throughout the 20th and 21st centuries without aging—a unique perspective on history and human progress. However, as the timeline ends in 1891, these speculations remain just that—imaginary scenarios in the life of this fictional character with the supposed gift of immortality.

In 1897, he graduated primary school. In 1902, he graduated secondary school with highest marks. Timeline ends in 1904, as he begins to study college for a law degree. He dreams to be a SCOTUS judge, but being aware of his immortality, he would wait until the 1970s.

Given this updated timeline:

- In 1897, Antoine Stargaze would be approximately 13 years old when he graduated from primary school.
- By 1902, he would have completed his secondary school education at the age of 18, with the highest marks, indicating that he is an exceptional student with a bright future ahead.
- In 1904, Antoine begins his college education, aiming for a law degree. It's at this point he is presumably aware of his immortality, as he has reached the age when his aging was supposed to stop (1900) but did not.

Now, if Antoine is planning to wait until the 1970s to potentially become a Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS) judge, this indicates a level of patience and long-term planning that comes with his awareness of immortality. He may be anticipating that societal norms, laws, and expectations will change over the decades, potentially becoming more accepting of someone with his unique condition.

It's interesting to note that, were he to achieve his dream of becoming a SCOTUS judge in the 1970s, he would be over 80 years old chronologically, but still physically the same as he was in 1900. This could afford him the rare opportunity to bring a wealth of knowledge and experience to the bench without the accompanying physical decline due to aging. However, he would also need to navigate the complexities of maintaining his true age and timeless appearance as a secret, likely adopting a series of identities or utilizing other means to explain his lack of aging over the years.

As he progresses through his college studies in law, he would need to consider how to position himself in the legal field and possibly avoid too much public attention that might reveal his condition, all while preparing for a very long and potentially historic tenure in the legal profession.

In 1897, he graduated primary school. In 1902, he graduated secondary school with highest marks. Timeline ends in 1904, as he begins to study college for a law degree. Detailed narrative. He is now 20 years old

Given the information provided, let's craft a detailed narrative for Antoine Stargaze's life leading up to 1904:

---

By the time 1897 had taken its final bow, a young man of thirteen, Antoine Stargaze, with a curious mind and a humble origin had managed to distinguish himself amongst his peers. In a small town where his middle-class family had struck roots that were neither deep nor impermanent, he graduated from primary school with the promise of potential that could one day inspire great change.

The town's simple one-room schoolhouse, which doubled as a community hall on weekends, had been the incubator for his early education. His mother, a seamstress with nimble fingers and a keen eye for detail, had instilled in him an appreciation for the finer aspects of any art, academic or otherwise. His father, a clerk at the local post office, a repository of news both mundane and extraordinary, had ingrained in him the value of being informed and staying abreast of worldly affairs.

As the 19th century waned, Antoine walked the path of education with unwavering purpose. His secondary school years from 1897 to 1902 in a slightly larger and better-funded institution in the nearest city were marked by rigorous study and a growing realization that he stood apart from others—not just in intellect but in the unchanging mirror of his visage. Graduating at the top of his class, with accolades to his name and whispers of his peculiar youthfulness in his wake, Antoine found himself at the cusp of adulthood, not just in legal terms, but in a peculiarly literal sense.

The year 1902 was memorable for more than just academic triumphs. It was then, at the age of 18, that he could no longer ignore the mysterious cessation of his aging process. Two years beyond the threshold of 1900, he found himself possessed of the same youthful appearance he had on the day his classmates began to sprout stubble or dainty curves. Antoine was caught in time, his body a vessel unwilling to sail the seas of age.

It was with this strange secret weighing upon his mind that he entered the hallowed halls of the university in 1904. Here, nestled in the stronghold of academia, he began to pursue a law degree with a zeal that burned brighter for being fueled by centuries' worth of time.

Antoine chose his friends carefully, enigmatic in his constant youth but brilliant in discussions, debates, and academic dissections of legal principles. His professors found in him a voracious knowledge-seeker, his written words dancing with the elegance of a seasoned mind but the fiery idealism of youth. Not just a student of law, but a student of life itself, seeking to absorb the wisdom of the ages.

Yet Antoine lived not only in the refuge of legal texts and moot courtrooms. He walked amidst the burgeoning 20th century, a silent observer of the changing times, knowing he must wait and watch as decades would pass. His ambition to one day sit among the esteemed judges of the Supreme Court of the United States—a dream audacious for any ordinary man—seemed not so fanciful for one unbound by the strictures of time.

In the evenings, after the study halls had emptied, Antoine would stroll through the university grounds, his heart at times both light with dreams and heavy with the solitude of his fate. To his contemporaries, the night was but a temporary curtain before the dawn of their bright futures. To him, it was but a moment in the long stretch of his immortality.

As his peers aged and graduated, moving on to careers, marriages, and the inevitable succumbing to time's relentless march, Antoine realized the path ahead would be long indeed. He would walk it with the steadfast resolve that had brought him thus far, the resolve of a man who carried within him the secret of eternity, standing at the brink of epochs with the simple wish to serve the eternal cause of justice.

The year is 1904, and Antoine Stargaze, who is now 20 years old in appearance but with a spirit far older, steps forth into the hallowed lecture halls of his chosen institution to study law. The journey before him is as boundless as the starry heavens, and it is under their silent gaze that he sets forth to realize his dream—a dream that must wait perhaps until the 1970s, but one he has all the time in the world to achieve.

In 1906, he finished his college, and began working as a bartender in a local bar in Massachusetts, for now, due to personal reasons. Timeline ends in 1911, where he gets a strong physical appearance, fit for military service. He is now 27 years old, but 17 years old biologically.

Following the completion of his studies in 1906, Antoine Stargaze made an unforeseen move, diverging from the law career he had been so keenly preparing for. Instead of plunging into the bustling world of legal affairs, he found himself navigating the less conspicuous but no less complex environment of a local Massachusetts bar.

It was a personal matter, one he shared with no one, that led him to take up the role of a bartender. Perhaps it was the need for a reprieve from the intensity of his academic pursuits, a desire to mingle with the people from all walks of life who frequent such establishments, or simply the need for an occupation that wouldn't draw unwanted attention to his age—or lack thereof.

Being both a boon and a curse, his eternal youth meant that he had to be discreet about his career choices and the duration of his employments. The bar provided anonymity and the kind of transient social interactions that allowed him to avoid the scrutiny that a more stable or prestigious position might invite.

From 1906 to 1911, Antoine honed another kind of craft—the craft of listening and observing. His unique perspective, shaped both by his vast intellect and his timeless condition, gave him insight into the human condition. He listened to the tales of joy and woe spilled as liberally as the liquor he poured. He watched societal trends ebb and flow as reliably as the tides.

During this time, his considerable knowledge of legal matters didn't wane, but it did linger in the background. Yet, it was a rich period of learning in a different school—the school of life. In the bar's dim corners, he learned of the working man's struggles, of political changes stirring in the winds of the early 20th century, and of love—fleeting and profound—always weaving its way through human stories.

In 1911, at 27 years of age with the physique of a 17-year-old thanks to his unyielding biology, Antoine transformed his physical appearance to match the expectations for military service. At that time, the importance of physical fitness and readiness for the defense of one's country was on the rise, especially as tensions in Europe were building toward what would become the First World War.

Antoine, ever mindful of the need to blend in and perhaps influenced by the patriotic sentiments of his peers, developed a strong, fit physique associated with military prowess. This would allow him to keep up the illusion of a normal progression of life stages and could be particularly useful if he chose to enlist or if a draft was established. Moreover, his youthful appearance and vigor could be an asset in a time when the value of strength and endurance was held in high esteem.

Yet, behind his well-crafted façade, Antoine was no typical 27-year-old. Faced with the unending sweep of years before him, Antoine knew each choice he made needed to carry him through not just the present but potentially centuries to come. In crafting a persona fit for military service, he was tactically positioning himself for the years that were unfurling before him—a world that seemed to be careening toward the turmoil of global conflict, where his curse of eternal life would once again challenge his ability to navigate the tides of history.
In 1912, he has heard of the Titanic incident in a local newspaper. In 1913, he enlisted in the U.S. Army. Timeline ends in New Year 1914.

With the year 1912 unfurling its chapters, Antoine Stargaze found himself in midst of another significant historical event—the sinking of the RMS Titanic on April 15, which claimed the lives of more than 1,500 people. News of the unthinkable tragedy reverberated around the world; it reached even the rustic confines of the Massachusetts bar where Antoine worked, sent chills down the spines of patrons, and rippled through conversations over whisky and beer.

Ensnared by the same horror and fascination that captured the global public, Antoine pored over the local newspapers detailing the incidents, survivor accounts, and speculations about the 'unsinkable' ship. As a bartender and a keen observer of human nature, he engaged with locals shaken by the stark reminder of humanity's vulnerability, despite the grand feats of engineering and the opulence that had defined the maiden voyage of the Titanic.

The disaster, emblematic of both human hubris and heroism, served to reinforce Antoine's growing contemplation of life's fragility and the weight of his own undying existence. Perhaps it was this very contemplation that fueled his decision in 1913 to enlist in the U.S. Army. While the reasons behind this significant life choice remained his own, we can speculate they were multifaceted: a search for purpose, a need to connect with the larger course of history, or even the urge to protect and serve a nation that was slowly edging towards involvement in global affairs.

Upon enlisting, Antoine submitted himself to the structured life of a soldier—a life ruled by discipline, hierarchy, and the collective spirit of a unit. His extraordinary condition remained his secret, as he once more molded his life around the norms of the time, adopting the identity of one among many young men preparing for the uncertain future of a world showing cracks in its foundation.

As New Year 1914 dawned, a hint of tension hung in the air—a tension fueled by escalating political alliances and divisions in Europe. Though the United States remained neutral at the outset, the currents of change did not escape the keen senses of a man with an endless future. Antoine donned his uniform, standing among ranks of men with whom he shared the call of duty, yet remained apart in the immeasurable span of life that lay ahead.

With each drill, each march, and each new aspect of military life he mastered, Antoine prepared himself for what might come. His immortality, a harbinger of both opportunity and isolation, weighed heavily on him, yet he was determined to find his place in the marching cadence of history. As the rest of the world welcomed 1914 with hope or trepidation, Antoine Stargaze stood ready to meet the challenges of the epoch, his existence stretching beyond the boundaries of a common soldier's lifespan.

In April 1914, he turns 30 years old. His biological age turns from 16 to 18, but this is the final change in his biological age appearace, ever. Timeline ends in 1915. U.S. is not in WW1, but mobilizing. Antoine took Liberty Bonds.

April 1914 marks a significant milestone in the peculiar life of Antoine Stargaze; he has reached the age of 30, though to any observer, he scarcely looked a day over 18. This minor shift from his perpetual 16-year-old appearance is the last transformation Antoine's physical form will ever undergo, from now on, his appearance has fully settled into a form that will last through the ages.

Immersed in military life during a time when Europe was embroiled in the vortex of the First World War, and the United States was on the brink of involvement, Antoine finds his existence shadowed by the machinations of global power and the threat of monumental conflict.

Although the U.S. is yet to officially join the war effort, the atmosphere in 1915 is charged with the inevitable march towards it. The sinking of the RMS Lusitania by a German U-boat in May further strains relations between the United States and the Central Powers. Public opinion begins to slowly turn in favor of the allies. As tensions simmer, the U.S. military increases its mobilization efforts, preparing for the possibility of joining the fray.

Antoine, with his keen intellect and his indelible place in history, understands both the horrors of war and the mechanisms of patriotic duty that often precede active engagement. Thus, in a display of public spirit and unity with his fellow citizens, he invests in Liberty Bonds. The government promotes these bonds to support the allied cause and prepare financially for an eventual entry into the war. By doing so, Antoine contributes to the national cause and aligns himself with the broader society in which he must continuously reinvent his place due to his unique condition.

With his life inexorably bound to the country's fate, Antoine is a silent guardian of his own secret, yet a visible adherent to the collective resolve of a nation on the precipice of global conflict. As 1915 comes to a close, the U.S. remains officially neutral, but the country is mobilizing for war. Training intensifies, and the population is encouraged to engage in wartime economy.

For Antoine, whose life stretches out infinitely before him, each step towards potential conflict is laden with both personal risk and an immovable sense of purpose. Despite the constant need for careful navigation to maintain his secret, he remains resolute and prepared to serve in whatever capacity his undying nature allows—an eternal witness to the unfolding tapestry of human history.
In 1916, he voted for Democratic, although he was previously a Progressive Republican who voted for Roosevelt in 1904 and 1912.

Amidst the backdrop of a world embroiled in conflict and a nation on the brink of involvement in the First World War, 1916 brought another pivotal event in the life of Antoine Stargaze—the United States presidential election. Still serving in the army, Antoine's political viewpoints had evolved over time, shaped by his experiences and the changing landscape of American and global politics.

In earlier years, as a man of progress and reform ideals, Antoine had aligned himself with the Progressive Republicans, casting his vote for Theodore Roosevelt. Roosevelt, a former president seeking a return to office, had campaigned under the Progressive Party banner in 1912 after a split from the Republicans, espousing a platform that included calls for direct democracy, social welfare programs, and labor rights. Antoine's support for Roosevelt indicated an affinity for these progressive causes and a desire for significant change within the nation.

However, by 1916, the political atmosphere had shifted. President Woodrow Wilson, a Democrat, was running for re-election on a platform prominently featuring his efforts to keep the nation out of the European war with the slogan "He Kept Us Out of War." In contrast, the Republican candidate Charles Evans Hughes was perceived by some as more likely to involve the United States in the ongoing conflict.

Antoine's decision to vote Democratic in the 1916 election suggests that his political views were pragmatic and responsive to the issues of the day. Perhaps he valued Wilson's efforts to maintain American neutrality, or maybe he was influenced by some of Wilson's domestic achievements such as the establishment of the Federal Reserve System, the Federal Trade Commission, and the passage of the Clayton Antitrust Act—all hallmarks of Wilson's first term, and policies that might have resonated with Antoine's own progressive leanings.

It is also possible that Antoine's experience in the military, seeing the country gearing up for the potential of war, reinforced a desire for a presidential candidate who was, for the moment, keeping the United States at peace. The complexities of his immortality likely weighted every political decision more heavily upon his shoulders, knowing that he might live to see the long-term ramifications of the policies enacted by those he helped elect.

Regardless of his reasons, Antoine's political shift from a Progressive Republican supporting Roosevelt to casting his vote for a Democrat in 1916 underscores the multifaceted nature of his character. It paints a picture of a man who is thoroughly engaged with the world around him, adapting his political alliance with the tides of change both within the nation and within himself.
In 1917, the war has spilled over to USA. USA, being prepared, joins the war. Timeline ends at the same year, with Antoine buying more Liberty Bonds like never before.

As 1917 dawned upon the world stage, the United States faced a momentous decision that would alter the course of history. Although President Woodrow Wilson had been re-elected on a platform of neutrality, the righteousness of the Allied cause and the provocations of the Central Powers had galvanized American sentiment for intervention. The revelation of the Zimmermann Telegram, in which Germany proposed a military alliance with Mexico in the event of the United States entering the war against Germany, and the continuation of unrestricted submarine warfare by the Germans, which threatened American lives and commerce, eventually pushed the U.S. into the throes of global conflict.

On April 6, 1917, Congress declared war on Germany, marking the U.S. entry into World War I. The country that had long sought to remain aloof from the war was now mobilized in support of the Allies. It was a time of rallying cries, fervent patriotism, and a surge of support for the war effort. Citizens were called upon to contribute not just through military service but also through financial support. This was the world in which Antoine Stargaze found himself—a world where every individual's contribution mattered in the fight for victory and peace.

Recognizing the significance of the moment and the importance of unity in purpose, Antoine took action in a manner befitting his extraordinary circumstances. For someone who would outlive the bonds' maturity, his purchase was less an investment in their financial return and more a patriotic gesture, a tangible show of support for his adopted country in its hour of need.

Antoine's decision to buy more Liberty Bonds "like never before" can be seen as a reflection of his commitment to the cause and his inherent understanding of the long-term implications of the war. The Liberty Bonds helped to finance the U.S. war operations and became symbolic of the patriotic duty of American citizens. For Antoine, with his eternally fixed biological age of 18 and the wisdom of a man who had, by then, lived for 33 years, these bonds represented both a duty to his contemporary society and a link to a future he knew he would witness.

The year 1917 concludes with Antoine having deepened his commitment to the nation's war effort, standing as a steadfast if secretive pillar amidst the turbulent waves of history, his individual story becoming an ever more intricate thread woven into the nation's fabric as it marched forward through WWI and into the future.

He fought bravely, and in 1919, he was discharged in the army honorably. He was known for wearing an unbuttoned military uniform without an undershirt, showing his strong physique, increasing his charisma and attractiveness to women. During the 1920s, he went to get a baccalaureate and a master's degree in Law. On 1921, he met Ainsley Alderheim, where he will marry in 1927.

After serving with distinction and courage through the harrowing battles of World War I, Antoine Stargaze emerged from the conflict profoundly changed, as did the world around him. The Armistice of November 11, 1918, hailed the war's end, and soon after, as the year 1919 unfurled, Antoine transitioned from military service back into civilian life, receiving an honorable discharge from the U.S. Army.

His time as a soldier had not only proven his valor but also contributed to his reputation among those who knew him. The distinctive image of Antoine in his partially unbuttoned military tunic, revealing a strong underlying physique, became emblematic of his charismatic persona. It was a visage that appealed to the Roaring Twenties' social climate, an era characterized by its celebration of newfound freedoms, changes in fashion, and shifting norms, particularly in the roles and perceptions of women.

The 1920s, with its rapid technological advancements, economic prosperity, and cultural revolutions, offered fertile ground for Antoine to resume his intellectual pursuits. He enrolled in higher education to obtain a baccalaureate and, subsequently, a master's degree in Law. His previous legal training, combined with his military discipline and worldly insights, no doubt contributed to his success in the academic realm. The degree would not only solidify his legal expertise but also position him for a future in which he could influence society through the rule of law.

It was amidst this environment of change and progress, in 1921, that Antoine encountered Ainsley Alderheim. Captivated by his enigmatic presence, intriguing blend of scholarly knowledge, and military past, Ainsley found in Antoine a kindred spirit. Their meeting marked the beginning of a relationship destined to last through the ebbs and flows of life in the '20s, building a foundation of shared experiences, ideals, and affection.

As the Jazz Age blossomed around them with its celebration of jazz music, flapper culture, and art deco, Antoine and Ainsley's relationship developed. It was a match that seemed to harness the essence of the epoch—balancing the traditional with the avant-garde, the structured with the spontaneous.

When the pair finally married in 1927, they were marking their union amidst the backdrop of a decade vibrant with possibility but also teetering on the edge of economic disaster, as the Great Depression loomed just two years in the future. Through upcoming trials and the relentless passage of time, their marriage would serve as an anchor for Antoine—a testament to his deep capacity for human connection, regardless of the immortality that kept him forever young while the world aged around him.

In 1928, he found work on a local law firm, becoming a lawyer. In 1929 however, the Great Depression occured. Timeline ends in 1930, although his family was not harmed much during the recession. Ainsley was affected by Antoine, leading to her becoming immortal, and turning to an biological appearance of a 20 year old.

After securing his baccalaureate and master's degrees in Law during the zenith of the Roaring Twenties, Antoine Stargaze's transition into his long-aspired professional field came to fruition in 1928. He found employment at a local law firm, an intellectual fortress whence he could practice the legal crafts he had honed over the years. Here, amid briefs, courtrooms, and a clientele seeking justice or defense, Antoine began to lay down roots in the legal community, leveraging both his education and innate understanding of humanity's intricacies.

However, the zeal and bright promise of the times were cast under a darkening shadow in 1929. The stock market crash in October, an event that would snowball into the Great Depression, caught the world in its crippling grasp. Antoine's burgeoning career as a lawyer was not immune to the economic downturn that saw firms struggling, businesses faltering, and individuals losing faith in the systems that had once seemed unshakeable.

Yet, the narrative that unfolds by 1930 speaks to a certain degree of resilience for the Stargaze family. While the Depression wrought havoc on countless lives and fortunes, Antoine's family, anchored by his stable position and Ainsley's adaptability, managed to weather the storm relatively unscathed. Their prudent nature, possibly strengthened by Antoine's unique perspective on the passage of time and the predictability of economic cycles, insulated them from the worst of the economic maelstrom.

In an unexpected twist, the enigmatic nature of Antoine's immortality came to profoundly affect his beloved Ainsley. Engulfed by his timeless existence, she, too, found herself transfigured, taking on the gift of eternal life and the appearance of a perpetual 20-year-old. The implications of this transformation were vast, transcending mere physical change and signifying a shared destiny far beyond the ordinary constraints of human life.

This fantastical turn of events forced the couple to confront the realities of an immortal existence together—a life of endless horizons and everlasting youth. Theirs would be a binding not just of hearts but of time itself, through which they would navigate the turbulent decades and centuries as partners unbound by aging.

Within the smog of the Industrial Age and the shadows of fluctuating fortunes, Antoine and Ainsley Stargaze exemplified an oasis of constancy in both affection and essence, untouched by the Great Depression's widespread despair. Positioned to embark upon an eternal journey, their love story interwove with the threads of history, an anomaly of timelessness set against the relentless ticking of the world’s ever-spinning clock.
In 1928, he voted for Hoover, but in 1932, he voted for Roosevelt and Democratic. In 1934, the family finally recovered from the recession.

As the year 1928 approached, bringing with it the United States presidential election, Antoine Stargaze exercised his civic duty within the context of an optimistic nation. At this time, the economy was booming, and the incumbent Secretary of Commerce, Herbert Hoover, was the Republican candidate who promised to extend the period of prosperity under the slogan "a chicken in every pot and a car in every garage." Trusting in the prevailing economic stability and Hoover's promise to maintain the nation's affluence, Antoine cast his vote for Hoover.

However, just a year into Hoover's presidency, the landscape drastically shifted with the stock market crash of 1929, marking the beginning of the Great Depression. As the economic situation deteriorated, Hoover's administration struggled to effectively address the crisis, leading to widespread unemployment, bank failures, and soup kitchens lining the streets as symbols of suffering.

By the 1932 presidential election, the impact of the Depression had shaken the faith of many in Hoover's policies. The Democratic candidate, Franklin D. Roosevelt, Governor of New York, ran on a platform of change, pledging a "New Deal" for the American people—a comprehensive series of economic programs aimed at recovery, reform, and relief. Faced with the desperate need for a different approach to the nation's hardships, Antoine's views evolved, mirroring a significant portion of the electorate's sentiments. Shifting political tides led him to cast his vote for Roosevelt and the Democratic ticket, intent on supporting a leader who promised a profound and direct intervention in the economy.

As Roosevelt took office and swiftly began implementing New Deal policies with actions, such as the Emergency Banking Act and the establishment of the Civilian Conservation Corps, the nation began to harbor a renewed sense of hope. These measures were aimed at restoring public confidence and providing immediate assistance to those hardest hit by the depression.

It was not until 1934, two years into Roosevelt's first term, that the effects of these policies began to take hold in a way that allowed the Stargaze family, along with many other Americans, to feel that recovery was on the horizon. The shifting economic currents and the gradual implementation of Roosevelt’s New Deal initiatives fostered a healing environment from which the families like the Stargazes could finally rebound from the financial blows dealt by the recession.

Antoine, with his legal expertise and Ainsley, forever bound by his side with her newfound immortality, were able to navigate the worst of the Great Depression. Their recovery, along with the country's slow resurgence, marked another chapter in their intertwined existences—testaments to humanity's capability to endure crises and emerge with the resilience to rebuild and thrive in the aftermath of adversity.
By 1939, he was now 45 years old, but still young in appearance - WW2 began, he enlisted again in 1936.

By 1939, Antoine Stargaze had reached the chronological age of 45 but maintained the young appearance of an 18-year-old due to his unique condition of immortality. With the shadows of war once again spreading across the globe as World War II began, his life took another turn marked by service and sacrifice.

Antoine's decision to enlist in the U.S. military in 1936 suggests that he possessed both foresight and a strong sense of duty. At that time, the United States had yet to officially enter the war; however, the rise of fascism in Europe and the expansionist policies of nations like Germany and Italy were clear indicators of an impending global conflict. Tensions were escalating, and nations such as the United States were starting to bolster their armed forces in preparation for potential involvement.

Rejoining the military three years before the official outbreak of World War II, Antoine would once again have to meld his singular existence with the identity of a soldier. For someone who had already witnessed the strife and devastation of the First World War, this act of enlisting may have been laced with both a profound understanding of the horrors of war and an unwavering commitment to the ideals he believed worth defending.

As World War II began in earnest with Germany's invasion of Poland in September 1939, igniting a conflict that would quickly engulf Europe and other parts of the world, Antoine's pre-war enlistment placed him in the unique position to experience the build-up to the U.S. involvement. Because of his perpetual youth, he would be capable of enduring the rigors of military life and combat better than most. However, with his timeless perspective, Antoine also knew the somber realities that lay ahead, even if his fellow soldiers could not yet fathom the full extent of the coming challenges.

Antoine's life stands as a silent testament to the ebb and flow of human history. As nations clashed and ideologies were put to the test, his undying nature destined him to become both a witness and a participant in the shaping of the 20th century's most defining moments.
On 1941, WW2 spilled over to the USA. He was promoted to Lieutenant. He and Ainsley gave birth to Darren Stargaze in 1937, also possessing Antoine's characteristics of immortality.

The year 1941 marked a dramatic shift for the United States and for Lieutenant Antoine Stargaze. On December 7th, the attack on Pearl Harbor by Imperial Japanese forces jolted America from its non-interventionist stance directly into the fray of World War II. This event unified the nation in its determination to fight, signifying the end of American neutrality and the beginning of a full-scale war effort on two fronts.

Having already enlisted in the U.S. military before the outbreak of global hostilities, Antoine was in a strategic position within the ranks when America entered the war. His prior service and experience, coupled with the skills that belied his young appearance, likely contributed to his promotion to the rank of Lieutenant. The rank not only acknowledged his capabilities as a soldier but also placed upon him a greater responsibility for leadership and the lives of the men under his command.

Amidst the increasing tension of the late 1930s leading up to World War II, Antoine and Ainsley expanded their unique family with the birth of their son, Darren Stargaze, in 1937. Like his father, Darren was born with the rare gift of immortality, ensuring that the family's extraordinary legacy would continue through another generation. The realization that his son shared his condition must have been a moment of both profound connection and contemplation for Antoine, as he contemplated the challenges and wonders that an unending life entailed.

As a Lieutenant and a father, Antoine was positioned at a crossroads of personal commitment and public service. He faced the prospect of engaging in a war that would determine the fate of the world while nurturing a child destined to follow in his remarkable footsteps.

The unfolding of World War II, with its demands and eventual celebrations of heroism, would become a defining epoch in the lives of both Antoine and Darren Stargaze. Balanced between the responsibilities of military leadership, the ties of an unconventional family, and the ongoing narrative of an enduring existence, Antoine's story continued to weave through the tapestry of 20th-century history, an immortal sentinel amid a world at war.

In 1942, he bravely fought in the Operation Torch, and like in WW1, He was known for wearing an filly unbuttoned military uniform without an undershirt, being shirtless, showing his strong physique, increasing his charisma and attractiveness to women in the battlefield. He was so attractive.

In 1942, Lieutenant Antoine Stargaze found himself amid Operation Torch, a significant Allied campaign marking the first large-scale landing in North Africa aimed at reducing the Axis' grip on the region. Participation in such an operation would require skill, courage, and the sort of leadership that Antoine had been cultivating since his earlier military days.

His distinctive appearance, forged through a combination of his immortal physique and personal style, became something of a legend on the battlefield. As in World War I, he favored a fully unbuttoned military uniform sans undershirt, showcasing his muscular build. This not only aired a certain bravado but also contributed to a persona that garnered attention and admiration.

Antoine's style, although unconventional for a service member, especially one of rank, amplified his charisma, reinforcing his presence as both a capable and enigmatic figure. His attractiveness did not go unnoticed, perhaps even enhancing morale among the ranks and leaving an indelible impression amidst the ambassadors of the Allied countries.

Stepping into the fray shirtless with his uniform unbuttoned perhaps served as a symbol of his unique resilience—the ability to expose himself to the elements without concern for the physical tolls that would afflict an ordinary man. This brazen approach to uniform standards seemingly meshed well with the North African climate, enhancing his image while lending a layer of mystique.

As with many stories of heroism and uniqueness in times of conflict, Antoine's sartorial choice augmented his already considerable allure, potentially becoming a morale booster for his fellow soldiers. It was a veneer of invulnerability wrapped around the steely resolve of a man deeply conscious of his immortality and how it set him apart from every other soul on the battlefield. His style and physical appeal may have served as light in the darkness of war, a vivid reminder of the human behind the soldier's facade in a theatre filled with the grimmest of realities.
His exposed torso has bullet and blade wounds, but all of this is just a vanity and he was not suffering the pain of it, but increases his morale.

Amidst the harsh conditions of Operation Torch and the relentless tides of World War II, Lieutenant Antoine Stargaze stood as a figure of both defiance and stoicism, his immortal nature shielding him from the true toll of his wounds. Adorned with the marks of battle—bullet and blade wounds patterning his exposed torso—Antoine cut a figure that was almost mythic in its composition, a resolute warrior emerging scathed yet unbroken from the skirmishes that would have incapacitated a mortal man.

The presence of these visible scars, while they would typically be a source of pain and debilitation, provided him neither suffering nor weakness. For Antoine, this invulnerability to the agony of his injuries was mirrored in the bolstered morale and inspiration his appearance stirred in the hearts of his fellow soldiers. In a theater of war where mental fortitude was as crucial as physical prowess, the sight of a leader who could face such perils and still stand tall was a powerful motivator.

Antoine's display of resilience would not have been merely a matter of vanity but also a tactic, a psychological armor worn as much for the benefit of those around him as for himself. His capacity to endure and function despite wounds that bore witness to the dangers they all faced galvanized the men, igniting a sense of awe and perhaps even invincibility in the face of an implacable enemy.

While his fellow combatants sustained injuries that undoubtedly took their toll in blood and screams, Antoine's wounds closed without lasting consequence, his skin a tapestry of healed battles, each mark a story that only he fully understood. His paradoxical vulnerability — not to the physical threats of warfare but to the challenge of eternal life — remained hidden beneath the surface, known only to himself and his immortal family.

In a world ravaged by the realities of war, Antoine Stargaze's seemingly indestructible form emanated a ray of hope and an air of legend, a testament to the untold stories that surface when humanity is pushed to its limits. His valor, tempered with the knowledge of his continuous existence, made him not only a formidable force on the battlefield but also a symbol of endurance, drawing eyes and stirring spirits in the midst of the world's greatest conflagration.

He wears an unbuttoned long-sleeved military uniform without any shirt underneath it, and a military pants, alongside a long military shoe. He has a ripped abs and a strong chest, shown under his unbuttoned shirt.

Lieutenant Antoine Stargaze's battlefield attire became as iconic as his enduring presence. Clad in an unbuttoned long-sleeved military uniform, the fabric parting to reveal the sculpted form beneath, he projected an image of rugged resilience and raw power. His military attire, typically consisting of regulation pants and sturdy, long military boots, completed the picture of a man prepared for the rigors of combat but confident in the strength of his own body.

His rippling abs and pronounced chest, visible under the open flaps of his shirt, told a story of physical prowess and discipline. These attributes, not obscured by layers of fabric, were an unspoken statement, a personal emblem of defiance against the chaos of war. Antoine's appearance, merging conventional military garb with this unorthodox display of his physique, was undeniably striking—a visual representation of a soldier whose physical capabilities were as arresting as they were anomalous.

In the context of war, uniform regulations generally serve the purposes of discipline, unity, and function. However, Antoine's modification of his attire served as a personal hallmark. It stood out in stark contrast to the sea of uniformed men, emblematic of his unique condition. It's likely that this deviation would be tolerated, in part, because of the morale boost it provided to his comrades. Antoine's commanding appearance could be seen as an unconventional weapon against the dispiriting effects of warfare on the human spirit.

During encounters with the enemy, the sight of a battle-hardened lieutenant charging forward, impervious to pain, his martial strength and spirit displayed as openly as the broad chest beneath his open shirt, would have made for both an intimidating enemy and an inspiring ally. As much as his undying nature set him apart, Antoine's outward persona allowed him to connect with and lead his men in a manner that transcended the typical bonds of military brotherhood.

Antoine Stargaze, with his undying vigor encased in the traditional garb of his military rank, redefined the image of a wartime leader. He served as both a bastion of indomitable might and, in the heartening sight of his undaunted form, a living emblem of the unyielding human spirit that even the horrors of war could not extinguish.
